LAW AND MOTION 302, ULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DISCLOSURE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE AN EXPERT DISCLOSURE IDENTIFYING THE NON-RETAINED EXPERTS SHE ACTUALLY INTENDS TO CALL TO TESTIFY AT TRIAL ON OR BEFORE THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON OCTOBER 31, 2014. PLAINTIFF'S DISCLOSURE OF "CUSTODIANS OF RECORDS" AS EXPERTS IS IMPROPER. PLAINTIFF MUST NAME THE CUSTODIANS OF RECORDS IF SHE INTENDS TO CALL THEM AS EXPERT WITNESSES. SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,200 PAYABLE IN 30 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. NIKOLAUS W. REED, TEL. NO. 940-7766 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRADLEY A. RUSSI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3964 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff's Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions - Motion is Granted. Plaintiff is ordered to produce an expert disclosure identifying the non-retained experts she actually intends to call to testify at trial on or before the close of business on October 31, 2014. Plaintiff's disclosure of "custodians of records" as experts is improper. Plaintiff must name the custodians of records if she intends to call them as expert witnesses. Sanctions is granted in the amount of $1,200 payable in 30 days. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/ehg
